Brand Launch

How to Start a Hoodie Brand in 2026: Everything You Need to Know

✎ FABRIOZA Team 📅 July 1, 2026 ☕ 13 min read
Home / Blog / How to Start a Hoodie Brand in 2026: Everything You Need to Know

The hoodie is the single best product to launch a clothing brand. It's versatile, high-margin, and universally wearable. This guide covers everything you need to know to launch a successful hoodie brand in 2026 — from fabric selection to manufacturer choice to pricing strategy.

Why the Hoodie Is the Best First Product

Hoodies offer the highest profit margins in apparel. Manufacturing cost: $12-20. Retail price: $60-120. That's a 3-6x markup. Hoodies are worn year-round (layered in winter, alone in spring/fall), appeal to all demographics, and turn your customers into walking advertisements for your brand.

Step 1: Brand Positioning

Decide your niche before designing: Premium streetwear (400 GSM, minimal branding, $80-120 retail)? Budget-friendly basics (280 GSM, bold graphics, $40-60 retail)? Athletic performance (tech fabric, functional details, $50-70 retail)? Your positioning determines every subsequent decision.

Step 2: Choose Hoodie Specs

GSM: 340-380 GSM for premium streetwear. 280-320 GSM for standard retail. Fabric: French terry for structure, brushed fleece for softness. Fit: Oversized/boxy for streetwear, regular for general retail, slim for athletic. See our full GSM guide.

Step 3: Find the Right Manufacturer

For hoodies specifically, look for: knitwear specialization, experience with heavyweight fabrics, embroidery and puff print capabilities, low MOQ (50 pieces), and ISO 9001 certification. FABRIOZA manufactures custom hoodies from 50 pieces with all GSM weights.

Step 4: Design and Brand Identity

Your hoodie design can be minimal (small chest embroidery) or bold (large back print, all-over design). Consider: logo placement (chest, sleeve, back), decoration method (embroidery for premium, puff print for streetwear), and interior details (custom neck tape, branded drawstrings).

Step 5: Order Samples

Order samples from 2-3 manufacturers. Evaluate: fabric weight accuracy, color matching, construction quality, label placement, and overall feel. Wash test the sample — quality hoodies should not shrink more than 5% or pill after washing.

Step 6: Price Correctly

Manufacturing + shipping = COG. COG × 4-5 = retail price. Example: $18 manufacturing + $4 shipping = $22 COG. Retail: $88-110. Don't underprice — hoodie customers expect to pay for quality.

Step 7: Launch

Photograph your hoodie on real people (not just flat lays). Use Instagram and TikTok to build anticipation. Consider a pre-order model to gauge demand. Launch with 50-100 pieces and scale based on sales data.

First Production Run Size

50 pieces for testing. 100-200 for a proven design. Don't order 500+ until you have consistent monthly sales of 50+ units.

Premium vs Budget Hoodie Positioning

FactorPremiumBudget
GSM340-400250-300
FabricHeavy French terryStandard fleece
FitOversized/BoxyRegular
BrandingMinimal, embroideryBold, screen print
Retail Price$80-120$40-60

Frequently Asked Questions

What GSM should I choose for my hoodie brand?

340-380 GSM for premium positioning. 280-320 GSM for standard retail. 400+ GSM for luxury tier.

How much does it cost to start a hoodie brand?

$1,500-2,500 for 50 pieces including samples, production, shipping, and branding.

Should I do embroidery or screen print?

Embroidery for premium positioning. Screen print or puff print for streetwear. Both can work — choose based on your brand aesthetic.

What is the best hoodie silhouette?

Oversized/boxy is trending for streetwear. Regular fit has the widest appeal. Choose based on your target customer.

How do I price my hoodies?

Manufacturing cost × 4-5 = retail price. Don't compete on price — compete on quality and brand story.
